findings.detail: The first autoresearch branch should inherit a voted baseline so the autonomous loop starts from a publicly legible design rather than a hidden default.
findings.title: Initial architecture should be community-approved

findings.detail: Every tuning proposal should point back to the active architecture direction so later runs can explain which configuration family they belong to.
findings.title: Hyperparameter tuning needs explicit parent lineage

findings.detail: The run record needs a concrete direction identifier so the dashboard can connect votes, code changes, and model outcomes.
findings.title: Autoresearch runs should reference the winning proposal

goal: Let the community define the baseline autoresearch architecture and later steer tuning changes while every decision and run state is persisted with Filecoin-backed storage.

governance.description: Registered contributors propose Filecoin-backed research directions and vote on what autoresearch should run next.

summary: The community selected a compact baseline architecture, then approved a sliding-window tuning proposal for the next autoresearch run.
